fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp=False and skips clamping when clamp=True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: clamp == True or (not clamp and y__1 == y__1)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the branch body (clamping) should execute, so 'if not clamp' being True when clamp=False means clamping happens only when it should NOT — this check is FALSE on failing tests where clamp=True but no clamping occurs.
The then-branch (clamping logic) should only execute when clamp is False due to the inverted condition — on failing tests clamp=True so this branch is never entered.

spec.expr: not clamp or (y__3 >= min(y0, y1) and y__3 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the final y value should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; failing tests have clamp=True with extrapolated x, so y__3 falls outside this range.
y__2 (clamped value) is computed only inside 'if not clamp', so it is only defined when clamp=False; when clamp=True this code is never reached, meaning clamping never happens.
